_meta.purpose: Lossless handoff for poster generation, webpage build, and GitHub deployment — reusable as a template for any future STRIKERS research project.
_meta.next_chat_instruction: Load this JSON first. It contains everything needed to recreate or adapt the PM1/PM2 conference posters, the research landing webpage, and deploy to GitHub Pages. Read all sections before starting any task.

PM1_poster.title: Performance Medicine: a scoping review of integrated human performance systems and the need for conceptual architecture
PM1_poster.format: A0 portrait (33.11 x 46.81 inches) — NOTE: portrait not landscape

header.title_style: Bold, white, large — Performance Medicine: bold, remainder italic
header.subtitle: Italic, white, smaller
header.authors: All 18 authors, italic, small white text
header.right_logo: strikerselite sports injury group dublin — white text

column_1_sections.name: Introduction
column_1_sections.content: Performance Medicine describes the integration of health optimisation and performance enhancement across high-demand human systems. Related models are used in elite sport and tactical, military, and corporate settings, yet the field lacks an agreed definition, conceptual structure, and governing framework. Without these foundations the design, delivery, standardisation, evaluation, and development of PM is limited.
objectives_bullets: Map the literature relating to integrated health and performance systems
Examine how these systems are described and delivered
Determine whether a coherent conceptual structure for PM exists

column_1_sections.name: Methods

content_structured.Design: PRISMA-ScR scoping review of the literature
content_structured.Sources: PubMed, Scopus, ProQuest Central, DOAJ, Google Scholar
content_structured.Setting: Integrated health systems in high-performance groups
content_structured.Variables: Frequency, case mix, context associations, service design

column_1_sections.paragraph: A structured claim-evidence synthesis was applied across the included literature, with each claim anchored to identified sources and weighted by evidence type and strength

column_2_sections.name: Results: Literature Characteristics
column_2_sections.paragraph: Most sources were descriptive or practice-led rather than theory-building, reflecting an active but disparate field without clearly conceptual architecture.

column_2_sections.name: Results: What is Performance Medicine?
column_2_sections.content: Integrated systems for health and performance optimisation is widespread in elite sport, common military and tactical settings, and emerging in corporate and technical contexts e.g. healthcare, engineering, and technology sectors.
bullets: Integration of medical, technical and supporting disciplines
System coordination is important for success
column_2_sections.overlap_note: Overlap with, but distinctions from, related holistic fields (Table 1): Performance Medicine / Sport and Exercise Medicine / Lifestyle Medicine

full_width_section.name: Results: Performance Medicine and related fields
full_width_section.content: Comparison table — see comparison_table data in this handoff

bottom_sections.name: Conclusion
bottom_sections.content: Performance Medicine is widely practised but insufficiently defined. Lack of conceptual architecture limits standardisation, evaluation and best practice. Empirical study and multidisciplinary stakeholder synthesis is needed to: Define the field more clearly / Develop an integrated framework / Build operational tools for PM system design, delivery and governance.

bottom_sections.name: Scan to explore and contribute
bottom_sections.content: Scan the QR code to explore our interactive research page, and help shape the field with the Performance Medicine Stakeholder Survey (5 to 8 minutes). Your perspective matters.

footer.left: Specialist Treatment and Rehabilitation of Injuries in Kicking, Explosive and Running Sports

PM2_poster.title: A Performance Medicine System for Optimising Human Potential: developing a prototype conceptual framework and operational tools

header.title_style: Bold, white — A Performance Medicine System for Optimising Human Potential: bold / developing a prototype... italic

PM2_poster.layout: 2 columns top (Introduction + Conclusion), full-width Results, 3-column bottom (PM-NEAT | PM-OpSpec | QR), footer

top_left.name: Introduction
top_left.content: Performance Medicine describes the integration of health optimisation and performance enhancement across high-demand human systems, but conceptual and structural gaps limit standardisation, evaluation, and best-practice design and delivery.
top_left.aim: Develop a conceptual framework and Operational Tools through structured multidisciplinary synthesis of scientific and applied literature

top_right.name: Conclusion
top_right.content: This work addresses an important gap by presenting a unified conceptual architecture and practical tools for Performance Medicine. The Performance Medicine Architecture, Operational Specification, and Need Assessment and Evaluation Tool provide a framework for system design, evaluation, and governance while establishing a basis for subsequent refinement, validation, and applied testing.

full_width_results.name: Results: The Performance Medicine Architecture
full_width_results.centrepiece: PMIF roundel figure — full size, centred

col1_PM_NEAT.full_name: PM Needs Evaluation and Assessment Tool
col1_PM_NEAT.strapline: Dual-purpose: prospective needs evaluation AND Current-state service review / gap analysis

section_A.title: A: Needs Evaluation and Planning
bullets: Assesses what a setting requires to align with PMIF and PM-OpSpec
Supports structured planning and service scoping
Identifies required capabilities, adaptations, and resources

section_B.title: B: Service Review and Improvement
bullets: Assesses how well current provision aligns with PMIF and PM-OpSpec
Identifies gaps, risks, strengths, and improvement priorities
Supports benchmarking, assurance, and quality improvement

col2_PM_OpSpec.strapline: Specifies what a PM system should be for a given setting: Requirements / Roles / Governance / Resources
bullets: Translates PMIF into context-specific operational requirements
Defines minimum standards and context-specific adaptations
Clarifies roles, remit, responsibilities, and jurisdiction
Specifies resources, governance, and evaluation expectations
Supports service design, commissioning, and planning

col3_QR.content: Scan the QR code to explore our interactive research page, and contribute to the Performance Medicine Stakeholder Survey

webpage.description: Single self-contained HTML file — all assets base64 embedded. No external dependencies except Google Fonts and Google Analytics.

tech_stack.CSS: Inline in <style> tag — mobile-first, CSS variables for palette
tech_stack.JS: Inline in <script> tag — vanilla JS only, no frameworks
tech_stack.fonts: Google Fonts — Inter + JetBrains Mono via CDN link
tech_stack.analytics: Google Analytics GA4 — replace G-XXXXXXXXXX with real Measurement ID
tech_stack.assets: All images base64 encoded inline — STRIKERS logos, St Andrews crest, PMIF figure, poster images, QR code

sections_in_order.content: PMIF roundel image (full width), domain annotation strips (2x2 grid of quadrant cards with all domain names), hero CTA button
sections_in_order.title: Performance Medicine (with 'Medicine' in OA purple)

sections_in_order.content: PM1 and PM2 paper cards — each with: tag, title, full verbatim abstract (all sections), poster View/Download/Share buttons, inline poster image drawer
10-row comparison table: PM vs SEM vs Lifestyle Medicine vs Preventive Medicine. Horizontally scrollable on mobile. PM column highlighted in OA purple.
2x2 quad grid (purple backgrounds), OA three-panel strip below
Three arch-cards: PMIF (conceptual), PM-NEAT (download PDF+DOCX), PM-OpSpec (download PDF+DOCX + inline 12-page reader)
QR code block, primary CTA button (Take the survey), secondary row (Share survey / Share page / Email / LinkedIn)

nav_structure.left: STRIKERS typemark logo | grey 1px divider | St Andrews crest

footer.logos: STRIKERS typemark | grey divider | St Andrews crest
footer.text: Both institutions / IOC UKCCIIS 2026 / CI name and email / development caveat

animations.scroll_reveal: IntersectionObserver — opacity + translateY on .reveal elements, threshold 0.08
animations.drawers: CSS display toggle — poster and OS drawers open inline below their card
animations.scroll_cue: Animated vertical line + 'scroll' label fadeIn at 2s delay

mobile_optimisations: viewport meta with user-scalable=yes for pinch-zoom on poster images
min-width:0 on nav-left to prevent overflow
all drawers use max-height + overflow-y:auto with -webkit-overflow-scrolling:touch
comp-table min-width:640px with overflow-x:auto parent for horizontal scroll

key_patterns.share_api: navigator.share() on mobile, navigator.clipboard.writeText() fallback on desktop
key_patterns.lazy_loading: loading='lazy' on all poster and OS page images

github_deployment.deployment_method: GitHub Pages — free tier, public repo required

api_push_method.description: Large files exceed GitHub API get_contents limit. Use git blob API for read/write.
read_steps: POST /git/trees with base_tree + new blob entry
POST /git/commits with message, new tree, parent commit
api_push_method.note: Always use Auth.Token() not deprecated login_or_token

make_repo_public_if_needed.step1: github.com → sign in → go to the repo
make_repo_public_if_needed.step2: Settings tab → scroll to bottom → Danger Zone
make_repo_public_if_needed.step3: Change repository visibility → Make public → type repo name to confirm
make_repo_public_if_needed.why: GitHub Pages requires public repo on free plan. GitHub Pro ($4/month) allows Pages on private repos.
make_repo_public_if_needed.alternative: Keep repo public (no sensitive data in it — all assets are already public via the live URL)

google_analytics_setup.status: GA4 snippet added to index.html — awaiting Measurement ID
google_analytics_setup.step1: Go to analytics.google.com → sign in with Google account
google_analytics_setup.step2: Create Account → Account name: STRIKERS Performance Medicine
google_analytics_setup.step3: Create Property → Property name: Performance Medicine Landing Page → select your country and timezone
google_analytics_setup.step4: Choose Web as platform → enter URL: andrewhallorthosports.github.io/performance-medicine → Stream name: PM Landing Page
google_analytics_setup.step6: Tell Claude your Measurement ID — Claude will replace both instances of G-XXXXXXXXXX in index.html and push to GitHub
google_analytics_setup.what_you_get: Real-time visitor count, country and city breakdown, device types, session duration, pages visited — all in your private Google Analytics dashboard

rating_system.RAG: >=2 Red / 1 Amber / <=0 Green / N/A if Need=0

nine_sub_components: C9 Required resources and enabling conditions
PM_OpSpec.maturity_reference_table: 9-row x 3-column table with descriptors for each level

poster_build_workflow.description: How to build a new conference poster using this template
steps: 1. Define paper type (scoping review / synthesis / RCT / etc) → determines header colour and paper_type tag
2. Populate the poster JSON structure (same schema as PM1_poster or PM2_poster above)
3. For PM1-style: 2-column layout with methods table and stat callouts
4. For PM2-style: 2-col top, full-width results with PMIF figure centrepiece, 3-col bottom tools
5. Header: always STRIKERS S mark + St Andrews crest + title + authors + contact + strikerselite right
6. Footer: always sponsor logos strip on white
7. QR code: always the live webpage URL
8. Export as PDF and add to GitHub repo for download

webpage_build_workflow.description: How to build a new research landing page using this template
steps: 1. Copy the section structure from webpage.sections_in_order
2. Replace all PM-specific content: titles, abstracts, domain names, figure, tools
3. Swap palette colours if the new project has a different visual identity
5. Update survey URL and QR code
6. Update nav links to match new sections
7. Add Google Analytics GA4 snippet with real Measurement ID
8. Push to GitHub using the blob API method in github_deployment.api_push_method
9. Enable Pages: POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pages with source branch main
reusable_components: Nav with logo + divider + institution logo pattern
Hero with figure + domain annotation strips
Paper card with abstract + poster drawer
Comparison table (adapt columns for new field)
Tool arch-cards with PDF/DOCX download + inline reader
Survey section with QR + share buttons
Share API pattern (Web Share + clipboard fallback)

standing_rules: No em dashes anywhere — use colons, commas, or parentheses
No AI or synthesis caveats within figures, forms, or posters — belongs in methods text only
No invented citations — every source externally verifiable
Conservative reasoning; explicit uncertainty; integrity above completeness
IN DEVELOPMENT label on PM-OpSpec form
Structural and architectural decisions are Andrew's to make; Claude executes faithfully
